markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://www.wired.com/story/the-righteous-ev-owners-who-wont-let-their-broken-cars-die/#main-content)

Comment12Save StorySave this story

Comment12Save StorySave this story

On an October evening in 2024, a gardener named Svein Hodne was driving home from vacation on a wind-buffeted coastal road in southwest Norway when his electric car began behaving strangely. Yellow and red warnings lit up its display. An alarm went off. The car lost power. Hodne barely managed to turn off the road and into a bus stop, right next to a graveyard and a church, before the car came to a stop. He was alone.

[Anduril Wants to Own the Future of War Tech. Mishaps, Delays, and Challenges Abound](https://www.wired.com/story/andurils-real-war-is-with-itself/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

From drones to missiles to submarines, the $30.5 billion defense startup wants to transform how the tools of war are made. It’s not all going as planned.

Paresh Dave

[![Two Literal Crypto Bros Built a Real Estate Empire. Then the Homes Started to Fall Apart](https://media.wired.com/photos/6995ca5e918558ad5998edb2/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/crypto-bros-built-a-real-estate-empire-then-the-homes-started-to-fall-apart/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Two Literal Crypto Bros Built a Real Estate Empire. Then the Homes Started to Fall Apart](https://www.wired.com/story/crypto-bros-built-a-real-estate-empire-then-the-homes-started-to-fall-apart/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

In 2019, two Canadian brothers blew into Detroit with an irresistible pitch: For $50, almost anyone could become a property owner. When houses decayed and the city intervened, the blame games began.

Joel Khalili

[![Opposing ICE Might Save the Country. It Could Also Ruin Your Life](https://media.wired.com/photos/69c5c6fcd03999521bd70374/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/opposing-ice-might-save-the-country-could-also-ruin-your-life/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Opposing ICE Might Save the Country. It Could Also Ruin Your Life](https://www.wired.com/story/opposing-ice-might-save-the-country-could-also-ruin-your-life/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

For months, lone vibe coder Rafael Concepcion has obsessively built tools to counter the federal immigration crackdown—pivoting as he’s been outmatched. He’s also lost his job and become a target.

Brendan I. Koerner

[![ICE Is Paying Salaries and More for This Town’s Entire Police Force](https://media.wired.com/photos/69c1ae3ecb79135fc6d9ad64/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/ice-is-paying-the-salaries-of-this-towns-entire-police-force/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[ICE Is Paying Salaries and More for This Town’s Entire Police Force](https://www.wired.com/story/ice-is-paying-the-salaries-of-this-towns-entire-police-force/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

Under a Homeland Security program, police departments around the US are signing up to assist in immigration enforcement. The cops of Carroll, New Hampshire, are going all in—and they’re likely not alone.

Sammy Sussman

[![Arm’s CEO Insists the Market Needs His New CPU. It Could Piss Everyone Off](https://media.wired.com/photos/69c2ef3f0355f9e1ff2fd1e1/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/arms-ceo-insists-the-market-needs-his-new-cpu-it-could-piss-everyone-off/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Arm’s CEO Insists the Market Needs His New CPU. It Could Piss Everyone Off](https://www.wired.com/story/arms-ceo-insists-the-market-needs-his-new-cpu-it-could-piss-everyone-off/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

Arm just confirmed the rumors: It’s producing its own chip for the first time. CEO Rene Haas explains why this won’t alienate the many chipmakers who license the company’s designs.

Lauren Goode

[![When Satellite Data Becomes a Weapon](https://media.wired.com/photos/69c3cf94af89d6230b241472/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/when-satellite-data-becomes-a-weapon/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[When Satellite Data Becomes a Weapon](https://www.wired.com/story/when-satellite-data-becomes-a-weapon/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

As war reshapes the Gulf, the satellite infrastructure the world relies on to see conflict clearly is being delayed, spoofed, and privately controlled—and nobody is sure who is responsible.

Gia Chaudry

[![Hassan Took a Bike Ride. Now He’s One of the Thousands Missing in Gaza](https://media.wired.com/photos/69bb3e4c52ccb057f936398b/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/hassan-took-a-bike-ride-now-hes-one-of-the-thousands-missing-in-gaza/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Hassan Took a Bike Ride. Now He’s One of the Thousands Missing in Gaza](https://www.wired.com/story/hassan-took-a-bike-ride-now-hes-one-of-the-thousands-missing-in-gaza/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

In a place denied access to basic forensic technology—and where people disappear into Israeli detention—the fate of thousands remains unknown. One of them is an autistic teenager.

Mahmoud Mushtaha

[![Don’t Expect Big Surprises in the Government’s Alien Files](https://media.wired.com/photos/699c832687b4ddbea4d1c67a/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/dont-expect-big-surprises-in-the-governments-alien-files/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Don’t Expect Big Surprises in the Government’s Alien Files](https://www.wired.com/story/dont-expect-big-surprises-in-the-governments-alien-files/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

Donald Trump has ordered the release of files related to aliens, UAP, and UFOs. If previous disclosures are any indication, get ready for a letdown.

Becky Ferreira

[![The WIRED Guide to Dumbo for Business Travelers](https://media.wired.com/photos/690d30c5fc40524223ebe1f4/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/the-wired-guide-to-dumbo-for-business-travelers/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[The WIRED Guide to Dumbo for Business Travelers](https://www.wired.com/story/the-wired-guide-to-dumbo-for-business-travelers/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

The Big Apple has had a tech boom in the past few years—here’s where to stay, play, eat, and work in the city’s tech-dense Dumbo neighborhood.

Farideh Sadeghin

[![Who Needs Tow Trucks? Portable Jump Starters Will Get You Home Without One](https://media.wired.com/photos/69cf3cac8743a73489a83876/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/best-portable-jump-starters/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Who Needs Tow Trucks? Portable Jump Starters Will Get You Home Without One](https://www.wired.com/story/best-portable-jump-starters/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

The new crop of portable jump starters means you'll never be stuck waiting on a tow. Here are the devices we'd trust.

Matthew Korfhage

[![This Is How Trump Is Already Threatening the Midterms](https://media.wired.com/photos/698bb2c3b6f5e1cacad97680/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/this-is-how-trump-is-already-threatening-the-midterms/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[This Is How Trump Is Already Threatening the Midterms](https://www.wired.com/story/this-is-how-trump-is-already-threatening-the-midterms/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

WIRED surveyed the ways the Trump administration is working to manipulate this year’s midterm elections.

David Gilbert

[![Your Vape Wants to Know How Old You Are](https://media.wired.com/photos/69c6b32db2e9941fa7bff508/16:9/w_640%2Cc_limit/undefined)](https://www.wired.com/story/your-vape-wants-to-know-how-old-you-are/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

[Your Vape Wants to Know How Old You Are](https://www.wired.com/story/your-vape-wants-to-know-how-old-you-are/#intcid=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_842461b4-48aa-4355-94bd-dbe57053428c_roberta-similarity1)

Companies hope that biometric age-verification tech in cartridges could put flavored vapes back in business. But it's unlikely to solve the real problems.

Boone Ashworth
